deathPlace.address: Tantallon Castle, North Berwick, East Lothian, Scotland

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
She is the daughter of William Hay of Yester and Alice Hay.

She married Sir William Douglas, 2nd Earl of Angus, on 3 Dec. 1414, having have been contracted to him on 12 Dec. 1410.

From 3 December 1414, her married name became Douglas. As a result of her marriage, Margaret Hay was styled as Countess of Angus circa 1425. A papal dispensation of marriage was granted in 1425. She died after 1484.

«b»Children of William Douglas, 2nd Earl of Angus and Margaret Hay«/b»
1.) James Douglas, 3rd Earl of Angus

2.) George Douglas, 4th Earl of Angus

3.) William Douglas

4.) Hugh Douglas

5.) Elene (or Helen) Douglas

zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Elizabeth Hay of Yester於1400年5月20日出生在英國蘇格蘭易亞斯特 洛迪安也斯特也斯特 卡素時，她的父親Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester25歲，母親Alice de la Haye18歲。她於1415年與William Douglas 2nd Earl of Angus在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1484年4月22日在英國蘇格蘭易亞斯特 洛迪安諾斯 貝維其可享年83歲。

nameMeanings.name: Hay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English (northern; of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in Normandy called La Haie or La Haye (Old French haie ‘hedge, enclosure’, ‘forest for hunting deer and other animals’, a borrowing of the ancient Germanic word haga). Robert de Haia or de la Haye is known to have come from La Haye-du-Puits in Manche; he was the founder of Boxgrove Priory in Sussex (1123), and holder of the Honor of Halnaker (Sussex) and (by marriage) the barony of Kolswein (Lincolnshire). The Norman name was also taken to Ireland, where it has since flourished in the county of Wexford as Hay and Hayes . Elsewhere in Ireland the name usually has a native Irish origin, see below.

English: topographic name from Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg) or ‘forest fenced off for hunting’ (Old French haie); or else a habitational name from a place so called, such as Hay (in Herefordshire and Westmorland) or Hey in Scammonden (Yorkshire). It was no doubt sometimes synonymous with Hayward .
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah).

variants.name: High
meanings: English:
nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hay 3.
topographic name for someone who lived ‘(on the) height or hill’, from Middle English heigh ‘height, hilltop’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hight .

variants.name: Hai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Abdul, Syed, Arifa, Atta, Faiz, Hamid, Iftekhar, Iskandar, Mohamed, Mohammad, Mohannad.  Vietnamese Mui, Pham, Tran, Vo, Bui, Cao, Chau, Dinh, Duong, Nguyen, Sau, Vo Van.  Jewish Ilan, Ronen, Shlomo.  Chinese Chan, Chiming, Lung, Wai Fun, Yin.
Muslim (mainly the Indian subcontinent): from a personal name based on Arabic ḥayy ‘alive’. Al-Ḥayy ‘the Living’ is an attribute of Allah.
Jewish: variant of   Hay  .

variants.name: Dehay
meanings: French: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone who lived by an enclosure, from the masculine form of Old French haye ‘hedge’; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word (see Hay 9).
Altered form (also De Hay) of Dutch De Haij or De Haaij, which is probably of French origin (see above).

variants.name: Haig
meanings: Scottish and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of various places in northern France named with Old Norse hagi ‘enclosure’, a word with cognates in most ancient Germanic languages. Compare Hay . The Haigs of Bemersyde in Berwickshire derive their name from the district of La Hague in Manche.
Scottish and Irish (Cavan): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Thaoig and Mac Thaidhg (see McCaig ).

variants.name: Hayman
meanings: English: topographic name probably from Middle English hai(e), hei(e) ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg), denoting a dweller by an enclosure (see Hay 2 ), or else a nickname for a ‘keeper of the enclosure’ (compare Hayward ).
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, high(e) (Old English hēah); see also Hay 3.
English: occupational name for a seller of hay, from Middle English hai, hei (Old English hēg, hīg).

variants.name: Hayes
meanings: Irish (Cork): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hAodha ‘descendant of Aodh’, a personal name meaning ‘fire’. Compare McCoy . In some cases especially in County Wexford, the surname is of English origin (see below), having been taken to Ireland by the Normans.
English: variant of Hay , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

English: topographic name from the plural form of Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (see Hay ), sometimes used as a collective noun for a farm, especially in Devon, where it is a frequent minor placename. Compare Hain .

variants.name: Haycock
meanings: English: from a pet form of the Middle English personal name Hai + coc; see Hay 4 and compare Cocke .

variants.name: Haylett
meanings: English (Norfolk): from a pet form (double diminutive) of the personal name Hai (see Hay 4), from Hai + -el + -ot.

variants.name: Hey
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hay  .
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized (or archaic) and Flemish, of Heij, a topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, Dutch hei, heide.
German: metonymic occupational name for a grower or mower of grass, from Middle High German höu ‘grass, hay’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Elizabeth Hay of Yester was born on 20 May 1400, in Yester Castle, Yester, East Lothian, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester, was 25 and her mother, Alice de la Haye, was 18. She married William Douglas 2nd Earl of Angus in 1415, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 22 April 1484, in North Berwick, East Lothian, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 83.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Elizabeth Hay of Yester est née le 20 mai 1400, à Yester Castle, Yester, East Lothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Alice de la Haye, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé William Douglas 2nd Earl of Angus en 1415, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 22 avril 1484, à North Berwick, East Lothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 83 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Elizabeth Hay of Yester am 20. Mai 1400 in Yester Castle, Yester, East Lothian, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester 25 und ihre Mutter Alice de la Haye 18. Sie hat 1415 in Scotland William Douglas 2nd Earl of Angus geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 22. April 1484 im Alter von 83 Jahren in North Berwick, East Lothian, Schottland, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Elizabeth Hay of Yester è nata il il 20 maggio 1400 nel Yester Castle, Yester, East Lothian, Scozia, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Alice de la Haye, aveva 18 anni. Lei sposato William Douglas 2nd Earl of Angus nel 1415, nel Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 22 aprile 1484, nel North Berwick, East Lothian, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 83 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Elizabeth Hay of Yesterが1400年5月20日にイギリススコットランドエアスト ロスアンイエスターイエスター カストルで生まれた時，父Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yesterは25歳，母Alice de la Hayeは18歳であった。1415年にスコットランドでWilliam Douglas 2nd Earl of Angusと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1484年4月22日にイギリススコットランドエアスト ロスアンノース バーウィックで83歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Elizabeth Hay of Yester(이)가 1400년 5월 20일 영국 스코틀랜드 이스트 로스이언 예스터 예스터 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice de la Haye(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 1415년 스컷랜드에서 William Douglas 2nd Earl of Angus(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1484년 4월 22일 영국 스코틀랜드 이스트 로스이언 노르스 버윅에서 83세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Elizabeth Hay of Yester nasceu em 20 de maio de 1400, em Yester Castle, Yester, East Lothian, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Alice de la Haye, tinha 18 anos. Ela casou-se com William Douglas 2nd Earl of Angus em 1415, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 22 de abril de 1484, em North Berwick, East Lothian, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 83 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Elizabeth Hay of Yester родилась 20 мая 1400 г. в местности иестир кастл, иестир, эаст лотян, Шотландия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir William Hay, Laird of Locherworth and Yester было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice de la Haye, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Douglas 2nd Earl of Angus в 1415 г. в местности Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 22 апреля 1484 г. в местности норт бирвикк, эаст лотян, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 83 лет.
